name: Antipasto Salad Platter
recipeIngredient: Basil - snipped fresh (1 tbsp) OR dried basil leaves (1 teaspoon)
Pasta - Radiatori or other variety (8 oz)
Garbanzo beans - canned and drained (20 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dressing first: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine olive or salad oil (1/2 cup), salt (1 t), crushed red pepper (1/8 tsp), garlic clove (1), lemon juice (1/4 cup), black pepper (1/4 tsp freshly ground), and basil - snipped fresh OR dried basil leaves (1 tbsp). Shake until well combined.
2. Cook the pasta: In a large kettle, bring 3 quarts of water, salt, and salad oil to a boil. Add pasta and return to boiling. Continue cooking uncovered, stirring occasionally with a long fork to prevent sticking, just until tender (about 7 to 8 minutes). Do not overcook. Drain well; do not rinse. Turn into a large bowl and add the dressing; toss to combine.
3. Cool completely.
4. To the pasta mixture, add cubed red pepper (1/2 cup), cubed green pepper (1/2 cup), cubed provolone cheese (1/4 lb), drained garbanzo beans (20 oz), sliced salami (1/4 lb), pitted black olives (1/4 cup), salad oil (1 tbsp), and chopped parsley (2 tbsp). Toss lightly to combine.
5. Turn into a serving bowl; refrigerate covered for 1 hour.
